segments.title: iSol Talent Acquisition Agreement — Bridging Recruiting Gap to UKG
segments.summary: HR presents item 10, an agreement with iSol Talent Acquisition (formerly Applicant Pro) to bridge the recruiting gap until UKG Pro goes live. Indeed recently cut the city's free job postings from unlimited to 3 per month, with 90 vacancies to fill. iSol provides access to Indeed plus 300+ job boards with 2-3 day setup. Councilor Caldwell offers to partner on distributing job openings to local community workforce organizations like Northeast Community Center.
processing_notes: Department of Permits and Development detection is false positive; actual presenting department is Human Resources
HR presenter not identified by name
iSol is bridge solution until UKG Pro recruitment module deploys

segments.title: Midland Avenue Paving Project — $620K Budget Overrun, TIP Funds Unavailable
segments.summary: City Engineer Mary Robinson presents items 11-13 for the Midland Avenue paving project (Ostrander Ave to West Seneca Turnpike). Bids came in $620K over budget due to a one-year delay from NYS DOT staff shortages and additional curbing. Local funds cover the shortfall, bringing total to $5.286M. Funding remains 71% federal, 13% Marchiselli, 16% local. Low bidder is Nardozi. Completion expected this year.
processing_notes: Marchiselli refers to NYS Marchiselli aid program for local road projects
TIP (Transportation Improvement Program) funding unavailable this cycle

segments.title: SPD 1153 Parking Design — Pump Track Relocation and Westside Trail
segments.summary: Mary Robinson presents items 14-16 for engineering design of two new parking lots at 115 South Geddes and 1171 West Fayette/Magnolia plus pedestrian access across an abandoned railroad bridge ($150K bond). Eric Annis (chief development officer) addresses community concerns about eliminating the existing pump track at Light Park — commits to building a new, improved pump track nearby alongside the Westside Trail connecting downtown to the near westside and Tip Hill. Councilor Caldwell suggests alternative parking near Ukrainian National Home that was previously dismissed as too far but is the same distance. Administration agrees to investigate before Monday.

political_signal.description: Councilor Caldwell pushes back on pump track elimination, suggests alternative parking site that administration has not fully evaluated

segments.title: Parks Omnibus — Breanna Stewart Kirk Park Donation, Landscape Contracts, and Community Programs
segments.summary: Commissioner Aisha Bird presents multiple parks items. Item 17: $50K Breanna Stewart Foundation donation for Kirk Park basketball court upgrades (lighting, rims, backboards); Stewart may visit Syracuse. Items 18-23: six landscape architectural service agreements at $300K each (5 of 6 local, two-year contracts, project-based). Item 24: Cornell Cooperative Extension 4H summer camp. Item 25: $20K ash tree treatment (148 trees). Item 26: Barks and Rex contract extension ($21K increase). Commissioner highlights upcoming improvements: Homarus concession/splash pad, playground investments, Thornden Park stage. Caldwell requests tick/deer awareness signage in parks. Resolution honoring Carmelita Sap Walker for Common Council service; all councilors join.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

segments.title: Pi Kappa Epsilon Donates $13,280 for Firefighter Behavioral Health Programs
segments.summary: Fire Chief Mike Maz presents item 28: acceptance of $13,280.52 donation from Pi Kappa Epsilon fraternity (Syracuse University chapter) for the Fire Department's Behavioral Health Assistant Program and Critical Incident Stress Management teams. Funds support training firefighters to identify and support colleagues experiencing psychological or mental health issues after difficult calls.

segments.title: Assessment Platform Migration — Image Mate to Schneider Geospatial Beacon
segments.summary: CIO Vinnie Scipion presents item 30: migration of Image Mate Online to Beacon (Schneider Geospatial) for the Assessment Department. Platform was acquired and must align with Onondaga County's migration timeline. Implementation costs waived ($14K value), annual cost ~$8K. Commissioner Matt OJ (Assessment) notes the platform gets ~250,000 hits/year and is used by every municipality in Onondaga County. Councilor Caldwell asks about local vendor alternatives; Assessment confirms predecessor SDG was local and responsive. Council discusses broader philosophy of aligning with county platforms on a case-by-case basis.

segments.title: Railroad Lease Extended to 10 Years, Assessment Corrections, Out-of-State Plates, and School Zone Camera Data
segments.summary: Multi-item segment. Item 33: Michael Layman (Assessment deputy commissioner) amends the South Geddes Railroad lease to extend from 1 to 10 years with right of first refusal. Item 34: Assessment role corrections for senior exemptions and a shelter rent property at 1445 South Salina. Item 35: Vicky Bos (Finance deputy commissioner) presents $8K agreement for out-of-state DMV plate lookups for parking ticket enforcement, which generates more revenue than cost. Councilor Jones-Rowser reports stolen/abandoned vehicles with out-of-state plates in her district. Extended discussion on school zone speed camera program: only June-July statements reconciled due to vendor reporting issues and disputed bank fees. Councilor Caldwell requests committee meeting with data on ticket volume, payment compliance, and camera relocations. Cameras do not operate during school breaks. Vendor (Florida-based) handles collections but city added local cash payment option.

political_signal.description: Council requests data and committee meeting on school zone speed camera program performance and vendor reconciliation issues

segments.title: HUD CDBG Action Plan, Senior Tax Exemption Expansion, and Federal Compliance Concerns
segments.summary: Three major topics. (1) Michelle Spansky presents items 37-38: HUD Year 52 annual action plan for CDBG, ESG, and HOME entitlement grants; public meeting drew ~15 attendees with 10 speakers. (2) Assessment Commissioner presents senior citizen property tax exemption expansion under NYS RPTL Section 467: extends eligibility to those earning up to $46,999.99 with up to 65% partial exemption. Staff processed 2,800 applications last year. Councilor Caldwell has championed this for months. (3) Councilor Ehrenreich raises concerns about Trump administration requiring immigration documentation from CDBG-funded agencies. Deputy Commissioner Spansky and Commissioner Michael Collins (NBD) confirm current court injunctions prevent enforcement; agencies are NOT being asked to collect that information. Executive session with corporation counsel recommended for detailed legal discussion.

political_signal.description: Councilor Caldwell champions senior exemption expansion she has pushed for months; council also vigilant about protecting CDBG-funded agencies from premature federal compliance

segments.title: Special Use Permit, EV Charging Grant Missed, and Good Cause Eviction Hearing Announced
segments.summary: Multi-topic segment. (1) Jake Dishaw presents item 44: special use permit for neighborhood market at 401 Pond Street, approved by planning commission with conditions for storefront improvements. Councilor Nave voices conditional support. (2) Item 45: Parkside Commons zone change R5 to MX2 approved by planning commission to eliminate split-zone parcel. (3) Extended EV charging follow-up: Commissioner Michael Collins reports the JCOM grant opportunity was missed because the RFP requirement could not be met in time. Owen Kernney contacted alternative vendors but none could provide the full package. One ongoing grant remains but requires ~$170K city match. Councilor Nave expresses disappointment at lost revenue. Caldwell asks if JCOM could still execute; Collins confirms yes but notes unresolved agreement issues. (4) Good Cause Eviction public hearing announced for tomorrow in town hall format with sign-up cards; Councilors Jones-Rowser and Paniagua to co-chair. (5) Code enforcement concerns raised about trash at West Gifford liquor store and parking at Brighton/Canon corner store.

political_signal.description: Council grapples with consequences of EV charging RFP decision; Councilor Nave expresses disappointment at missed revenue opportunity while respecting equity concerns

processing_notes: JCOM grant deadline passed; city missed $400K+ infrastructure opportunity due to RFP timeline conflict
Owen Kernney contacted alternative vendors per council request; none could provide full service package
Good Cause Eviction public hearing scheduled for Feb 19 in town hall format
Amanda (council secretary) departing for county position after earning master's degree

departments_found: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs
